rulings.Case Title: CARMEN BIMINCHUMO VS. KAMEL KARAJAH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Defendants' Discovery Request
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT KAMEL KARAJAH DBA THAT'S IT GROCERY Motion To Compel Responses To Defendants' Discovery Request. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The defendant's motion is GRANTED. No opposition was filed. The plaintiff shall serve verified responses to the defendant's Request for Production of Documents, Set Number Two within 10 days. The defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $584.00 is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY AND FRANCES GAGE LP VS. ROGELIO NORIEGA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT ROGELIO NORIEGA's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted in part and denied in part. The request to set aside the default pursuant to CCP section 473 is denied as untimely. CCP sec. 473(b) provides that such a motion must be made within six months after the entry of default. The default was entered on November 20, 2013. Defendant did not file his motion to set aside the default until July 9, 2014, beyond the six month period. Further, Defendant did not attach a proposed responsive pleading to his motion, as required by CCP sec. 473(b). The request to set aside the default judgment entered June 17, 2014 is granted. Plaintiff's request for attorneys fees and costs pursuant to CCP 473(a)(1) is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TIARA BILLUPS LARKIN, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. AYLSSA GUTNER-DAVIS, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Terstominy At Depo And Verified Responsesto Disco Requests
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT AYLSSA GUTNER-DAVIS, Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Testimony At Deposition And Verified Responses to Discovery Requests. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The defendant's motion is GRANTED in part. No opposition was filed. The plaintiff shall serve verified responses to Form Interrogatories-General, Set One and Request for Production of Documents, Set One within 10 days. The motion to compel the plaintiff to attend and testify at deposition is DENIED. It does not appear from the papers filed that the plaintiff has failed or refused to appear and testify at deposition. The defendant's request for sanctions in the amount of $580.00 is GRANTED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CUI XIA RONG VS. ZHI YAN PAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT ANNA MAU's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the second cause of action for Violation of the Unruh Act, the demurrer is overruled. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads all of the requisite elements of the cause of action - (1) that defendant denied full and equal services to the plaintiff; (2) that a substantial motivating reason for defendant's conduct was its perception of plaintiff's race; (3) that plaintiff was harmed; and (4) that defendant's conduct was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff's harm. (CACI 3060; FAC, pars. 24-25, 27, 37-39.) The FAC adequately pleads facts establishing that Plaintiffs have standing, as the FAC alleges that they were denied services due to their race. California cases have "allowed parties that are not clients, patrons, or customers in the traditional sense to bring Unruh Act claims." (Strother v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (9th Cir. 1996) 79 F.3d 859, 873; see e.g. Jackson v. Superior Court (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 936.) As to the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The FAC does not plead facts establishing "conduct so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in a civilized community." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 494.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JHONA MATHEWS VS. THE ARCHDIOCESE OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTS MONSIGNOR JAMES TARANTINO, CAPUCHIN FRANCISCAN ORDER OF CALIFORNIA - WESTERN, THE ROMAN CATHOLIC ARCHBISHOP OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the first cause of action for sexual harassment in violation of FEHA, the Demurrer is sustained as to Defendant Tarantino with leave to amend to allege, if Plaintiff can in good faith, Defendant Tarantino's status as an employer. If Tarantino is an employee of Defendant Archbishop of San Francisco ("ASF"), the exclusion for religious corporations in Gov. Code section 12940(j)(4)(B) applies to exclude ASF's employees from FEHA liability as a matter of law. (Gov. Code section 12940(j)(3).) Further, the demurrer is also sustained because the Complaint fails to allege sexual harassment by Tarantino personally. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for sexual harassment in violation of the Unruh Act. The Unruh Act does not apply in employment or quasi-employment contexts. (See, e.g., Alcorn v. Anbro Eng'g, Inc. (1970) 2 Cal.3d 493, 500 [the Act applies only to business establishments "in the course of furnishing goods, services or facilities to its clients, patrons or customers"].) All of the alleged activity here arises in the employment context and therefore the Complaint cannot be amended to cure the defect. As to the fourth and fifth causes of action for negligent supervision and negligent hiring and/or retention against Defendant Tarantino, the Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege, if Plaintiff can in good faith, Defendant Tarantino's status as an employer. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend the conspiracy to commit sexual harassment cause of action in light of the rulings on the demurrers to the FEHA and Unruh Act causes of action. As to the 10th cause of action for conspiracy to commit fraud, the Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to allege facts supporting each element of the cause of action with the requisite specificity. The Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. The SAC alleges that Defendant Tarantino knew about the extreme sexual harassment and abuse, and failed to act, and that he monitored and observed the sexual conduct using the video cameras in the church. Such behavior, if proved, could be deemed extreme and outrageous. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA WOODS VS. CBRE GROUP, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration On An Ind Basis And To Dismiss With Prejudice/Alt To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT CBRE GROUP, INC's Motion To Compel Arbitration On An Ind Basis And To Dismiss With Prejudice/Alt To Stay Proceedings Pending Arbitration. This motion is off calendar as moot. A First Amended Complaint ("FAC") was filed on July 1, 2014. "Because there is but one complaint in a civil action, the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint." (State Comp. Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) The Court notes that Defendant has already filed a motion to compel arbitration directed at the FAC that will be heard on September 16, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/MJM)
